metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_973
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 2: Proprioceptive sensory neurons that synapse onto motor neurons in the spinal cord receive axo-axonic inputs from GABAergic inhibitory interneurons or GABApre neurons. Loss of NB-2 resulted in a decrease of GABApre boutons onto sensory presynapses.

12/10/2017 Pim
- The study uses a general Contactin-5/NB-2 KO making the site of action (pre/postsynaptic) unclear. However, in Fig.1 the authors show that Contactin-5/NB-2 is only expressed by proprioceptive sensory neurons and not by motorneurons. The site of action or Contactin-5/NB-2 in synapse organization must therefore be presynaptic. In Fig.2 a positive correlational relationship between level of presynaptic Contactin-5/NB-2 expression and the number of boutons in line with this idea.. Experimental description: "mouse strains were used in this study"
